017package org.apache.juneau.rest.server.tracing;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.StringUtils.*;
020
021import java.io.*;
022import java.util.logging.*;
023
024import org.apache.juneau.http.response.*;
025import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.*;
026import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.processor.*;
027
028/**
029 * Writes the W3C Trace Context response headers ({@code traceparent} and, when present,
030 * {@code tracestate}) so a client calling this server can read the resulting trace identifiers back
031 * off the response.
032 *
033 * <h5 class='topic'>How it works</h5>
034 *
035 * <p>
036 * A {@link TracerHook} bridge (e.g. the OpenTelemetry bridge in {@code juneau-rest-server-tracing-otel})
037 * renders the W3C {@code traceparent} / {@code tracestate} values from the <i>server-started</i> span
038 * context at {@link TracerHook#startSpan(RestRequest) span-start} time &mdash; the only point where
039 * that context is reliably active &mdash; and stashes them as the request attributes
040 * {@link #ATTR_TRACEPARENT} / {@link #ATTR_TRACESTATE}. This processor runs later, during response
041 * rendering (after the span scope has already closed), reads those stashed strings, and writes them
042 * as response headers.
043 *
044 * <p>
045 * This capture-at-span-start / write-at-response-time split is deliberate: the framework opens and
046 * closes the tracing scope around the {@code @RestOp} handler in
047 * {@link RestOpInvoker#invokeOp(RestOpSession)}, which completes <i>before</i> response processors
048 * run, so {@code Span.current()} is no longer valid here. Keeping the rendered values on the request
049 * also keeps this processor free of any OpenTelemetry (or other tracer) dependency &mdash; it works
050 * with any {@code TracerHook} bridge that populates the two attributes.
051 *
052 * <h5 class='topic'>Off-by-default behavior</h5>
053 *
054 * <p>
055 * The first action in {@link #process(RestOpSession)} is a single request-attribute read. When no
056 * non-no-op {@code TracerHook} ran for the request, no {@link #ATTR_TRACEPARENT} attribute is present
057 * and the processor returns {@link #NEXT} immediately with no allocations &mdash; matching the
058 * off-by-default, zero-cost contract of the observability hooks. Registration of this processor is
059 * itself gated on {@code RestContext.responseTraceparent} (default {@code true}); setting it to
060 * {@code false} keeps the processor out of the chain entirely.
061 *
062 * <h5 class='topic'>Already-committed responses</h5>
063 *
064 * <p>
065 * If the response has already been committed (for example a streaming / SSE handler that flushed its
066 * headers before returning), the headers can no longer be set. In that case the processor logs a
067 * {@link Level#FINE FINE} message and skips the write &mdash; it never throws.
068 *
069 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
070 *    <li class='jc'>{@link TracerHook}
071 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://www.w3.org/TR/trace-context/">W3C Trace Context</a>
072 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerObservability">REST Server &mdash; Observability (Micrometer + OpenTelemetry)</a>
073 * </ul>
074 *
075 * @since 10.0.0
076 */
077public class TraceContextResponseProcessor implements ResponseProcessor {
078
079   /**
080    * Request-attribute key under which a {@link TracerHook} bridge stashes the rendered W3C
081    * {@code traceparent} value (e.g. {@code 00-0af7651916cd43dd8448eb211c80319c-b7ad6b7169203331-01})
082    * for this processor to emit as a response header.
083    */
084   public static final String ATTR_TRACEPARENT = "juneau.traceparent";
085
086   /**
087    * Request-attribute key under which a {@link TracerHook} bridge stashes the rendered W3C
088    * {@code tracestate} value (a comma-separated {@code key=value} list). Only set when the active
089    * trace state is non-empty.
090    */
091   public static final String ATTR_TRACESTATE = "juneau.tracestate";
092
093   private static final String HEADER_TRACEPARENT = "traceparent";
094   private static final String HEADER_TRACESTATE = "tracestate";
095
096   private static final Logger LOG = Logger.getLogger(TraceContextResponseProcessor.class.getName());
097
098   @Override /* Overridden from ResponseProcessor */
099   public int process(RestOpSession opSession) throws IOException, BasicHttpException {
100      var req = opSession.getRequest();
101
102      // NoOp short-circuit: no active tracer stashed a trace context for this request.
103      var traceparent = req.getAttribute(ATTR_TRACEPARENT).as(String.class).orElse(null);
104      if (isEmpty(traceparent))
105         return NEXT;
106
107      var res = opSession.getResponse();
108
109      // Headers already flushed (e.g. streaming / SSE response) — body was also written; chain is done.
110      if (res.getHttpServletResponse().isCommitted()) {
111         LOG.log(Level.FINE, () -> "Response already committed; skipping traceparent/tracestate header injection.");
112         return FINISHED;
113      }
114
115      res.setHeader(HEADER_TRACEPARENT, traceparent);
116
117      var tracestate = req.getAttribute(ATTR_TRACESTATE).as(String.class).orElse(null);
118      if (tracestate != null && ! tracestate.isEmpty())
119         res.setHeader(HEADER_TRACESTATE, tracestate);
120
121      // Headers only — let the downstream processors render the body.
122      return NEXT;
123   }
124}
